meaning: adj.令人不安的,令人惊惶的
examples: She had the disconcerting habit of saying exactly what she thought.
The reception desk is not at street level, which is a little disconcerting.
This code is a little disconcerting.
And it is disconcerting when religious and ethnic parochialism is apparent in reformasi rhetoric.
But the research keeps revealing unexpected, sometimes disconcerting, evidence to explain just how true this is.
